events.title: Fun and Learn

This is a drop-in program. No registration required.

events.description: Author Sarabeth Holden (Sea Snooze; Benny the Bananasaurus Rex) reads from her new children's book Strawberry Sundaes leads a short story writing workshop. The audience and author will come up with words to base a story on, then orally tell a story from their new collection of words. Participants will be invited to then create their own story. ​This creative writing workshop is for children 3- 8.

The program is rooted in interactive storytelling of stories, folktales and drumming from Afrika as well as the Caribbean, the Americas, and around the world. This program is intended for all children and their families. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toron

events.title: Rainbow Day
events.description: Celebrate Pride Month at Toronto Public Library! Come celebrate Pride Month at our Rainbow Day – a fun-filled program that celebrates the 2SLGBTQ+ community with a rainbow-themed craft, games and music!  For families with kids 12 and under.  Drop-in program. Space is limited.  FREE tickets are available at 1:30 pm.

Children younger than 5 years of age will require caregiver

events.title: Movie Screening: Moana 2 (2024)
events.description: Join us for a movie on the big screen! Bring a friend, and a snack! Moana 2 (2024) 1h40min Rated: G Moana 2  is a musical animated adventure where an older, wiser Moana reunites with Maui and a new crew to sail dangerous, uncharted waters of Oceania. Three years after her first journey, she must break an ancient curse to connect her people with the rest of the world. *This program is suitable for all ages; younger children must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Drop in!*

events.title: Arts & Crafts
events.description: Let your creativity loose at Cliffcrest Library with our Summer Wonder Arts & Crafts program! We'll be making different projects every week in July & August. All supplies will be provided and you get to take your creations home! Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time at the Park
events.description: Drop in and us at Appletree Market in June Rowlands Park in the main market area for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 4:30  to 5pm . Weather permitting!

events.title: EarlyON Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 5 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment. This program is offered in partnership with the Malvern Family Resource Centre (MFRC) . This is a drop-in program. No registration required.

events.title: Wednesday Whimsy
events.description: For children ages 2-6 with their parents or caregivers. Join us for Wednesday Whimsy! Little ones ages 2-6 will enjoy a brand-new adventure each time. We'll spark imagination and learning through stories, crafts, songs, and playful activities. Get ready to wiggle, giggle, and discover something wonderful! No registration – but space is limited. Large groups and daycares cannot be accommodated.

events.title: BenTastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! For ages 0-12.

events.title: Magic Mel's Amazing Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as she leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fast-paced, comedy magic show for the entire family. Suitable for children 3 years old and up with their parent or caregiver. This program can hold up to 60 guests including adults with their children. When signing up pleas

events.title: Story Time & Craft
events.description: Join us for a fun and engaging storytime followed by a hands-on craft activity perfect for children ages 3-6 with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Chess and Board Game Club for Kids
events.description: Join us for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Chess sets and lots of other fun board games and puzzles are provided. Drop-in, no registration required. Limited spots available on a first-come, first-serve basis.

events.title: Movies @ Albert Campbell Library
events.description: Join us at the library this summer and watch a movie on the big screen! Bring a friend, and a snack! This program is suitable for all ages; younger children must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Drop in. August 27 - Kung Fu Panda 4 (2024). Run Time: 1h 34 min. Rated PG September 1 - The Cat in the Hat (2003). Run Time: 1h 22 min. Rated PG September 4 - Ratatouille (2007). Run Time 1h 51 min. Rated G.

events.title: Yoga Storytime with Appleseed Yoga
events.description: ​Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Appleseed Yoga for Kids will deliver inspiring stories that get the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materials required. Drop-in with limited space. All

events.title: Learn Not to Burn: Fire Safety for Children
events.description: Join the Toronto Fire Service in this special visit to teach our young community members about fire safety and prevention, and what to do when there is an emergency. Drop-in program for children and families. No registration required, space is limited.

events.title: Under the Sea BINGO
events.description: For ages 6 to 12.  Children under the age of 6 may attend but must be accompanied by a parent or caregiver.   Dive under the sea and celebrate the end of the summer with a game of ocean-themed BINGO! Kids will enjoy multiple rounds of play with underwater-themed cards, friendly competition and chances to win fun book prizes each round.

events.title: Summer Movies
events.description: Join us for Summer Movies on Thursday afternoons! August 6 - Zootopia 2 (2025, 108 min. G) August 13 - Finding Nemo (2003, 100 min. G) August 20 - Oceans (2010, 84 min. G) August 27 - Muppet Treasure Island (1996, 99 min. G) For children ages 6 and up. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.title: Fictional Character Bingo and TD Summer Reading Club Draw
events.description: Let's finish off this summer with a fun round of Bingo! This time, we'll be doing popular fictional characters in lieu of numbers. Have you read lots of stories? Maybe you can guess who they are too! Prizes will be given out for Bingo winners! Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 4 to 8. Space is limited. Registration begins June 1st. Information Collection Notice: The perso

events.title: EarlyON School Readiness
events.description: This program encourages the development of social skills, independence skills, communication skills, parental support skills, and early literacy skills. For parents/caregivers and their children ages 3-5 who are starting school in September of this year. Space is limited.

events.title: The Teddy Bears’ Picnic Storytime at Hubbard Park
events.description: Celebrate our last storytime at Hubbard Park with The Teddy Bears Picnic storytime! Bring your teddy bears and blankets and listen to the classic story, sing some songs and participate in fun activities! Weather permitting this program will take place at Hubbard Park (550 Gerrard Street East) or inside the Riverdale branch. For children of all ages with their parents or caregivers. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.title: Move with the Music with Nancy Kopman
events.description: Children's musical performer, Nancy Kopman, offers programs for younger children and their caregivers encouraging active appreciation of music and movement, using a mix of original and well-known easy songs. Ages 0-6. Drop-in.

Participants will be invited to then create their own story. ​This creative writing workshop is for children 3- 8.

events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

All children who register at our kick-off can enter their name for a chance to win a prize. Children can participate in a fun Scavenger Hunt to earn an extra ballot!  Drop-In any time from 2-4 PM.

events.title: Tizzy and Me: Fifteen Ways to Love a Mink
events.description: Join author and veterinarian Nadja Lubiw-Hazard for an interactive storytelling program as she reads from her children's chapter book, Tizzy and Me: Fifteen Ways to Love a Mink. The story is narrated by spunky seven-year-old Georgia, who lives with her two moms and their dog Tizzy. tells a story celebrating animal activism and 2SLGBTQ+ families. Following the reading, there will be a Q&A with the author and a storytelling activity using story stones.

events.description: Explore different STEM-themed activities every Sunday. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited. Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis.  Suitable for ages 6-12. Dash: May 3, June 7, July 5, August 9 LEGO: May 10, June 14, July 12, August 16 Snap Circuits: May 24, June 21, July 19, August 23 Sphero: May 31, June 28, July 26, August 30

events.title: Sunday STEM
events.description: Join us each week for a fun afternoon of exploring Science, Technology, Engineering and Math through small projects. Ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: SUNDAY FUN-DAY
events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Use our guides to build a creation or use your creativity to build what you want!

events.title: Chess and Board Game Cafe
events.description: Join us for a relaxed session of games and good company. We’ve got chess sets and a great selection of board games ready to play. Whether you're into strategy or just here for some laughs, all skill and interest levels are welcome! Drop-in (no registration required).

events.title: Sunday Fun in June!
events.description: Sunday Surprise! Drop in for some fun! Explore an hour of creativity and maybe some Snap Circuits, too!

events.title: Sunday Build & Play
events.description: Create exciting structures using Feltro tiles and KEVA blocks. For ages 4 - 12 .

events.title: Sunday Funday
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and game. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon for Kids - Mini Soccer Game
events.description: Craft, create, and have fun -- perfect for kids 6+ and their families!  Please register only the children who will be participating in the craft. Adults do not need to register.

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Follow creative prompts or use your imagination to create something fun and unique.

events.title: Rainbow Craft for Kids
events.description: Come and make a rainbow-themed craft. All materials will be provided. Drop-in. No registration required. For kids ages 6-12.

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Sunday Children's Programs
events.description: Join us every Sunday afternoon for a rotating serious of hands-on activities!  Age 6-12 . Drop-in. No registration required . June 7 - Sunday Lego Building Challenge June 14 - Sunday Snap Circuits June 21 - Sunday Lego Building Challenge June 28 - Sunday SLIME Making

events.title: Lego Builder's Club
events.description: A fun and creative space to play, learn, and build! Through hands-on LEGO challenges & teamwork activities. Space is limited, tickets will be handed out 30 mins before program starts.

events.title: Sunday Stories and Craft
events.description: A fun program for families to come together and enjoy stories and craft. Drop-In.

events.title: Sunday Snap Circuits
events.description: Learn all about electricity and circuits and try out some fun projects! For ages 6-12.   No registration required but space is limited.

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday Marble Run
events.description: Sunday Marble Run, a family-friendly event designed for all ages but ideal for children ages 6-12 and adults. Kids can challenge themselves by creating intricate mazes.  Families with younger children are welcome to participate as well, with adult supervision. Build, create, and enjoy together. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday STEAM at the Riverdale Branch
events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits, LEGO, K'nex, Marble Run and Kids Craft Club. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in.

events.title: Kids Crochet Corner
events.description: Join us for a staff-led beginner friendly crocheting program! No experience required. All materials will be provided. No Registration Required. Drop-In, limited capacity. Ages 6-12.   *Doors will close when capacity is reached.

events.title: Sunday Dash and Dots
events.description: Join us for a fun afternoon of robotics! Learn how to control, code and move your robots. events.description: Join us to make a fun new craft every week! For kids ages 4-12.  Drop-in. No registration required.

events.description: Join us for a Summer Movie every Friday!  Friday July 3 - Early Man (2018) Friday July 10 - Captain Underpants (2017) Friday July 17 - Snow White (1937) Friday July 24- Lego Movie (2014) Friday July 31 - Frozen (2013) Come to the library and cool off while enjoying some films. events.title: Wonder Workshop: Introduction to Design Thinking Building Challenge
events.description: Have you ever wondered what it would be like to be an inventor? Have you ever thought about building something yourself? What is something you would like to build? Is there a problem you would like to fix? Use your imagination and brainstorm creative solutions to develop a machine that will fix a problem or make a task easier for you! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6-12.

events.description: Experience the hit movies featuring supervillain turned secret agent, Gru and his group of colourful little minions this summer in our library's theatre! events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers.

events.title: Builders Club
events.description: Do you like to build with LEGO, DUPLO, Feltro, Snap Circuits or KEVA? Join us every Saturday morning for some fun building challenges! All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Caribbean Storytime with Yolanda T. Marshall
events.description: Let's get moving! Join Guyanese-born Canadian author Yolanda T. Marshall for a lively, interactive storytelling program as she reads from her Caribbean-inspired picture books followed by Soca music. Please note this program will be in place of our regular Saturday Family Storytime.

events.title: Saturday Craft
events.description: To celebrate your Saturday mornings, join us for a fun craft! Supplies are limited. First come first served.

events.title: Builder's Club

Donations in good condition are welcome, but not necessary. Feel free to drop off items any time on or before the event. Come out for coffee and meet your neighbours, library staff, and volunteers.

Comment couper le courant ? Une expérience électrisante nous aidera à élucider ces mystères ! Les enfants plongeront dans le monde fascinant des circuits électriques en construisant leur propre système lumineux. events.title: Recycled Papermaking with Paper Powered Workshops
events.description: Learn how to make environmentally-friendly, recycled, handmade paper in this fun, easy, and interactive workshop. Leave with sheets of beautiful, one-of-a-kind paper plus the knowledge that you did something great for the earth! Participants will learn about the ancient craft of papermaking, and have an opportunity to make their own beautiful, recycled paper from paper scraps, plus a variety of techniques to further customize their sheets. For children ages 9-12. Information Collection Notice: T

events.description: Discover what happens when science meets art! Explore perception as you use shadows, colour, motion, and light to create art from science. You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein) Please note: this program is suitable for children 4 years old and above with adult accompaniment. events.title: Builders' Club

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! events.title: Eww...that Science Is Gross!
events.description: Our guest Scientists in School help us discover the science behind things that make us go...ewww!  For example, learn about animals from their scat (ie. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag.  Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure! This program is for children 6 years to 12 years..  Space is limited.  Register in advance.

events.title: Saturday Movie: The Secret Garden
events.description: Presenting the film version of  The Secret Garden , published in 1911 by Frances Hodgson Burnett, a British/American beloved author of children's literature. This classic novel centers on the physical and emotional transformation of two sickly, neglected children through the healing power of nature and friendship. This is the 2020 Disney version of the film starring Colin Firth.  Everyone is welcome to this inclusive screening. Drop-in! No registration required.

events.title: Stomp, Chomp & Roar @ Dino Village

events.title: Kids & Parents Book Club
events.description: Our book selection for July is  The Wonderful Wizard of Oz by L. Frank Baum. Swept away from her home in Kansas by a tornado, Dorothy and her dog Toto find themselves stranded in the fantastical land of Oz. A true classic of children's literature, and enchantingly different from the equally classic film. Copies available at the library. The Wonderful Wizard of Oz

events.title: Water - Relay Educations STEM Workshop
events.description: Dive into our waterways and learn how we use water for drinking, sanitation, energy, and more. We talk about Indigenous understandings of water, and how cities and rural areas manage water supply in Canada. This workshop highlights the importance of the hydrological cycle. The hydrological cycle describes the movement and flow of water on Earth, and involves different stages such as evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Family Movie: Flubber
events.description: Rated G, approx. 94min Join us on a Saturday afternoon with Professor Phillip, Flubber & Weebo as they find different ways to have fun. Don’t forget the popcorn! Family, Drop-in

events.title: Screenwriting for Beginners
events.description: Have an idea for a movie, but not sure how to write it? Writing a screenplay can be tricky as the industry has strict rules on how they are formatted.This workshop will guide participants on how to properly format a screenplay and the different software available to make this process easier. Computers will be provided for this program. events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Engineering with Responsibility. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Paint Day!
events.description: Join us for a fun summer paint day! We'll begin with a brief lesson on acrylic and watercolour painting. After that, you can start crafting a beautiful masterpiece, or just have some fun experimenting with colours! All materials will be provided. Suitable for families and people of all ages. Dress for painting. Drop in!

events.title: Pride Fuse Bead Keychains
events.description: Celebrate Pride Month at Toronto Public Library by creating a Pride themed Fuse Bead Keychain to hang on your backpack or your jeans! events.title: Container Gardening: Supporting Our Local Pollinators in Small Spaces

Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Programming in Robotics This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Seniors Celebrating Seniors: A Musical Walk Down Memory Lane
events.description: Join Jerry and Peter for an engaging and nostalgic musical experience featuring a rich selection of beloved songs from the 1950s and 1960s. Accomplished multi-instrumentalists, they will perform on a variety of instruments including guitar, bass, violin, and accordion, bringing timeless classics to life. This interactive, one-hour program invites participants to reminisce and share personal memories connected to the music, fostering a warm and inclusive atmosphere. events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. *With the exception of June, where we will meet on the 4th Tuesday. This year we are reading:  June 23 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 15 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 17 -  Small Things Like These by Claire Keegan

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat
